blueprint.problem_statement: The EU lacks a dedicated regulatory framework for quantum technologies, resulting in fragmented investment, unclear standardisation, and insufficient coordination to compete with US and Chinese quantum programmes.
blueprint.baseline_description: Recovery scenario: EU GERD recovering post-shock, Horizon Europe quantum calls oversubscribed, Member States increasing national quantum budgets. Window of opportunity to lock in governance before market matures.

objectives.description: Capitalise on recovery momentum to establish durable quantum governance
Lock in investment trajectory with binding governance before market fragmentation
Scale quantum workforce rapidly during recovery window

policy_options.name: Baseline — no new intervention
policy_options.description: Allow recovery to proceed organically without new regulation.

policy_options.name: Coordination only — voluntary frameworks
policy_options.description: Use recovery momentum for voluntary coordination and roadmap alignment.

policy_options.name: Light regulation — standardisation and certification
policy_options.description: Establish standards and certification to channel recovery investment into interoperable EU quantum infrastructure.

policy_options.name: Comprehensive regulation — EU Quantum Agency
policy_options.description: Leverage recovery window to establish EU Quantum Agency with full governance powers.
